stem: Consider the Boolean expression Q = (A AND B) OR (NOT C) with three inputs A, B and C.
model_answer: Work out (A AND B) and (NOT C) first, then OR the two. Q is 1 if (both A and B are 1) OR (C is 0). This means Q is only 0 when A AND B is 0 AND C = 1. All eight rows: ABC 000 => Q=1 (NOT C=1); 001 => 0; 010 => 1; 011 => 0; 100 => 1; 101 => 0; 110 => 1; 111 => 1.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Complete a truth table with columns A, B, C, A AND B, NOT C and Q for every combination of inputs.

mark_points.text: correct column for A AND B (only 1 when A = 1 and B = 1)
correct column for NOT C (inverse of C)
Q = 1 whenever (A AND B) = 1 OR (NOT C) = 1
accepted_alternatives: applies OR of the two intermediate columns

mark_points.text: Q column values in order (A,B,C going 000..111): 1,0,1,0,1,0,1,1
